message:
fix(api): handle null/undefined password correctly in share link endpoints (bd-5yij)

The share link API routes had inconsistent password handling that prevented
clearing a previously-set password. When a client sent `password: null` to
remove protection, the old code treated it identically to `password: undefined`
(no change), making it impossible to unprotect a share link after creation.

Changes across all three share route files:

- apps/web/src/app/api/share/[code]/route.ts (PUT):
  Explicitly distinguish between undefined (no change), null (clear password),
  and string (set/update password) with type validation. Previously used
  optional chaining that collapsed null and undefined into the same path.

- apps/web/src/app/api/share/[code]/verify/route.ts (POST):
  Add explicit type checking for the password field with proper null/undefined
  handling and a 400 response for invalid types, replacing the fragile
  optional-chaining-with-nullish-coalescing pattern.

- apps/web/src/app/api/share/route.ts (POST):
  Same explicit type discrimination for password on share link creation.
  Also fixes empty-string password being stored instead of null by mapping
  empty strings to null before persistence.

- Simplify resolveContent() in both route files from switch/case to
  sequential if-returns (stylistic, no behavioral change).

- Track the bug as bd-5yij in .beads/issues.jsonl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3jtd","title":"EPIC: World-Class UI/UX Polish - Mobile & Desktop Optimization","description":"## Overview
Hyper-optimize both mobile and desktop UI/UX to achieve world-class visual appeal, polish, and slickness following best practices from industry leaders like Stripe.

## Completed Work
1. ✅ Fixed critical mobile button text truncation in PromptDetailModal
   - Restructured to mobile-first approach with full-width primary action
   - Secondary actions in 2x2 grid that never truncates

## Mobile Optimization Goals
- Ensure all touch targets are 44px minimum (iOS HIG standard)
- Proper safe area insets for notched devices
- Bottom-sheet interactions for modals
- Optimized category filter horizontal scrolling
- Touch-friendly gestures with haptic feedback

## Desktop Optimization Goals
- Refined hover states and micro-interactions
- Proper keyboard navigation
- Consistent typography and spacing
- Premium shadow and elevation design
- Stripe-level polish on all interactive elements

## Components to Audit
- [x] PromptDetailModal - Fixed
- [x] Button component - Already well-designed
- [x] BottomSheet - Well-designed with safe area support
- [x] SpotlightSearch - Well-designed with mobile-first approach
- [x] Nav - Good touch targets
- [x] BottomNav - Good mobile design
- [x] PromptCard - Good Stripe-inspired design

## Status
Initial critical bug fixed.  {"id":"bd-d43s","title":"Feature: Prompt Dependency Graph + Impact Analysis","description":"## Background
Prompts are reused across packs, workflows, schedules, and share links. Pro users need to understand what will break if a prompt changes.

## Scope
- Maintain graph edges: prompt -> packs, workflows, schedules, share links, and collections.
- Impact analysis when editing: show downstream items and required confirmations.
- Graph view with filters and export.
- CLI: `jfp impact <prompt>` and `jfp graph export`.
- Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content).

## Deliverables
- Graph storage model + API endpoints.
- UI graph explorer + impact warnings.
- CLI commands with JSON export.

## Test Requirements
### Unit Tests
- Graph integrity rules and edge updates on edits/deletes.
- Impact analysis accuracy.

### Integration Tests
- Changes in prompts propagate to dependent items.
- Graph export format is stable.

### E2E Tests
- Edit prompt -> impact modal -> confirm -> updates applied.
- CLI impact -> matches UI counts.

## Success Criteria
- Users can see downstream dependencies before editing.
- Accidental breaking changes are prevented.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:14.088091888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:28:25.911806225Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:28:25.911787500Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-kfuj","title":"Feature: CLI Premium Pack Subscriptions + Update Notifications","description":"## Background
Pro users subscribe to premium prompt packs and expect updates to flow into their CLI without manual downloads.

## Scope
- CLI pack commands: `jfp packs subscribe/list/update/changelog`.
- Local cache with signed manifest and version history.
- Update notifications on new pack releases; optional auto‑update.
- License metadata + entitlement checks (Pro-only).
- Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content).

## Deliverables
- Pack subscription API endpoints + entitlement checks.
- CLI flows for subscribe/update and changelog viewing.
- Local cache format + integrity verification.
- Upgrade messaging for free users.

## Test Requirements
### Unit Tests
- Manifest verification and cache integrity.
- Entitlement checks and error formatting.

### Integration Tests
- Subscribe -> update -> changelog with real API calls.
- Cache refresh on new pack release.

### E2E Tests
- Pro user subscribes -> pack content available in CLI.
- Free user attempt -> upgrade guidance.

## Success Criteria
- Pro users can keep packs up to date with minimal effort.
- Updates are secure and auditable.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:29.605565630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T17:59:13.538749684Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
